description: census designated place in North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Cherokee  (Cherokee: ᏣᎳᎩ, romanized: Tsalagi) is a census-designated place (CDP) in Swain and Jackson counties in Western North Carolina, United States, within the Qualla Boundary land trust.

description: county seat of Henderson County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Hendersonville is a city in and the county seat of Henderson County, North Carolina, United States, located 22 miles (35 km) south of Asheville.

description: unincorporated community in Transylvania County, North Carolina
description_wiki: Balsam Grove is an unincorporated community located in Transylvania County, North Carolina within Pisgah National Forest and Nantahala National Forest.

description: mountain range in the eastern United States and Canada
description_wiki: The Appalachian Mountains, often called the Appalachians, are a mountain range in eastern to northeastern North America.

description: coeducational public university in Cullowhee, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Western Carolina University (WCU) is a public university in Cullowhee, North Carolina, United States.

description: law school in St. Paul, Minnesota, U.S
description_wiki: The Mitchell Hamline School of Law is a private law school in Saint Paul, Minnesota, United States.

description: Native American people indigenous to the Southeastern United States
city in Knox County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Barbourville is a home rule-class city in Knox County, Kentucky, in the United States.
William Harry Frasier (May 5, 1908 – March 31, 2000) was the head coach for the Gonzaga University men's basketball team for the 1942–1943 season.
Richard F. Byrd (born April 30, 1953) is a retired American college basketball coach who served as the head coach of the Belmont Bruins men's basketball team from 1986-2019.
The Navajo are an Indigenous People of the Southwestern United States.

entity: Supreme Court of the United States
aliases: Supreme Court of the United States of America
description: highest court of jurisdiction in the United States of America
description_wiki: The Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) is the highest court in the federal judiciary of the United States.

description: archaeological site in Swain County, United States of America
description_wiki: Kituwa (also spelled Kituwah, Keetoowah, Kittowa, Kitara and other similar variations) or giduhwa (Cherokee: ᎩᏚᏩ) is a Woodland period Native American settlement near the upper Tuckasegee River, and is claimed by the Cherokee people as their original town.

description: federally recognized tribe of Cherokee people
description_wiki: The Eastern Band of Cherokee Indians (EBCI), (Cherokee: ᏣᎳᎩᏱ ᏕᏣᏓᏂᎸᎩ, Tsalagiyi Detsadanilvgi) is a federally recognized Indian tribe based in western North Carolina in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) is an executive department of the United States federal government that aims to meet the needs of commercial farming and livestock food production, promotes agricultural trade and production, works to assure food safety, protects natural resources, fosters rural communities and works to end hunger in the United States and internationally.
